entries.title: odoo-ai-skills
entries.description: The local-first verification & deploy gate for AI-written Odoo 17/18/19 changes — static indexes suggest; this verifies against the running instance. Read ground truth from the live instance first (fields, MRO/super chains, views, security, runtime flow, runtime state + exception post-mortem, reverse field impact, effective per-user/company security, module load state), build the smallest correct change, prove it with tests, and review it before merge — never guess. Layer-I enforcement gates turn that evidence into proof: risk-based scenario test generation, dev-vs-prod environment parity, a static anti-pattern validator, enforced privacy redaction, an upgrade/migration harness, a deploy approval gate, a one-command evidence bundle (PR-comment verdict), and a bring-your-own-index claim verifier; plus a local Odoo developer-docs index (Layer J). No SaaS, no seats, no metadata leaves the box. Covers models, scaffolding, views, OWL, public web (controllers/website/portal), security, reports, data, migration, performance, deploy (incl. odoo.sh), and domain playbooks.

entries.title: dev-loop
entries.description: Eight autonomous agents — PM, QA, Dev, Sweep, Reflect, Ops, Architect, and Director — that run a software-development loop coordinated entirely through ticket state. Five are inward / build-facing; three are OUTWARD agents (conventions §21): Ops watches RUNNING prod over time and files Urgent `incident` Bugs on a confirmed+repeated degradation (anti-flap re-check + dedupe, never auto-rollback); Architect audits whole-codebase technical health on a rotating, SHA-gated dimension and files `tech-debt` Improvements (read-only, never implements); the Director owns DIRECTION — on the optional `service` hub it chairs a cross-agent DISCUSSION BOARD (opens topics, the role-lens agents post per-round perspectives, the Director synthesizes → a decision) and DRAFTS the kind:"roadmap" doc that the OPERATOR publishes, folding optional real-user `signalSources` (PII-safe; graceful no-op without a `director` config; conventions §25). Ops/Architect observe-and-file; the Director coordinates + drafts; none implements, ships, verifies, or auto-applies a structural change (§17 — a structural ask stays a `[director-proposal]` ticket). PM proposes and verifies features from a strategy doc; QA hunts and re-tests bugs; Dev pulls Todo tickets in priority order, implements, self-reviews, ships, and smoke-checks prod (auto-revert on a break) — and can OPTIONALLY split into a two-tier Dev (conventions §21a): a senior-dev (opus) that authors a living per-module design doc and delegates staged child tickets to a junior-dev (sonnet) that implements them, with the senior-dev direct-coding any escalated verify-fail; the split is per-project and opt-in (launcher DEV_SPLIT + PM routing) and the legacy single dev agent stays active so non-split projects are unaffected; Sweep is a lifecycle janitor that fixes tickets falling through the cracks (missing/wrong owner labels, orphaned In Progress); Reflect is a daily retrospective that studies the loop's own behavior and curates the per-operator lessons.md from recurring evidence (drafting, never auto-applying, structural changes). A one-time `init` setup command (not a loop agent) bootstraps a product into the loop with a DETECT → MAP → ASSEMBLE → LOAD flow — it detects the project shape (greenfield / brownfield / adopting; single- or multi-repo), read-only-maps a brownfield codebase into a fixed-heading PM doc-base, runs a greenfield strategy interview when there is no code yet, gathers config (incl. extra repos), provisions Linear labels/project (and one `repo:<name>` label per repo when multi-repo), scaffolds the strategy doc/test env/runtime files, optionally adopts named pre-existing human tickets with per-ticket operator confirmation, and prints a readiness checklist. The loop coordinates one or many repos: tickets target a repo via a `repo:<name>` label, with per-repo build/branch/deploy resolution (single-repo stays 100% unchanged). Every ticket the system touches carries a `dev-loop` label so it never disturbs the human backlog. Coordination runs through a configurable backend — Linear (default) or a machine-local file board (`backend:"local"`) with the same state machine and protocols. Every agent also writes daily/weekly/monthly reports to the data dir and acts on any operator review (点评) of them — distilling the critique into a per-agent lessons.md rule that improves its working method (conventions §22). Reports default to machine-local files; an opt-in reports.sink:"linear" (conventions §23) instead hosts the report body and the 点评 channel in Linear for cloud/remote runtimes (default-off, with §16 guardrails and channel-split review provenance). An OPTIONAL Codex companion (conventions §24, via the codex-plugin-cc plugin + the codex CLI; absent OR disabled OR no codex on PATH ⇒ 100% unchanged) gives the loop an advisory second-model review (Dev Step 5.5 + Architect), native image generation (PM mockups + Dev production assets — the one capability the agents lack), and a one-shot rescue before a fix-exhausted block — all gated per sub-flag, advisory only, and never touching Linear. When a ticket is left human-parked for the operator (blocked+needs-pm+external-prereq), PM pings you out-of-band via an optional Slack/Lark webhook (conventions §9 `notify`; default-off, announced once, secret-safe). The coordination backend is pluggable (conventions §18): Linear (default), a machine-local file board, or — new — a local `service` hub (an MCP system-of-record over node:sqlite, zero native deps; see docs/HUB-ARCHITECTURE.md) that gives the loop real PER-AGENT IDENTITY (every write attributable, not Linear's single shared user), versioned operator-published DOCUMENTS, the §25 DISCUSSION BOARD + Director, an optional poll-based two-way IM CHANNEL (Lark/Slack — the operator chats with the Director; §16-secret-safe, no daemon), and an optional ONE-WAY Linear MIRROR (the hub pushes its tickets to Linear for human visibility; split-brain enforced — Linear is never read back as truth), and is CLI-PORTABLE (the same agents + hub run on a second coding CLI — Codex/opencode — against the same db, gated on a per-CLI identity-propagation test; docs/PORTABILITY.md); opt-in, Linear + Claude Code stay the default.

entries.title: sidecar
entries.description: Project-agnostic AI coding sidecar — SELF-CONTAINED Claude Code plugin: governance hooks (guards + injects) AND the sidecar CLI itself (bin/ · cli/ · lib/ · modules/ · config/ · templates/ · styles/) ship as ONE unit. Hooks run the plugin's own bundled CLI via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, so `/plugin update` + reload refreshes everything together — no per-project copy, no separate `sidecar self-update`. Slash commands ship as BARE `/cmd` via `sidecar shadow` (user-scope ~/.claude/commands), NOT plugin-namespaced — see `commands: []` below. Falls back to a global `sidecar` on PATH when present.

entries.title: kernel
entries.description: Claude Code learns from itself. KERNEL gives your agent persistent memory (AgentDB), multi-agent orchestration (15 agents, tiered routing), and a scientific experiment engine that proves which rules actually work. Your agent remembers failures across sessions, coordinates parallel work without merge conflicts, and evolves its own methodology through hypothesis testing. 20 skills, 14 commands, auto-adapting project profiles. The plugin stays general-purpose — project-specific session overlays live in the consuming repo's own hooks, not here.

entries.title: metate
entries.description: Full development pipeline as Claude Code skills: prep -> build -> review -> smoke -> aftercare -> ship. The implementer (cursor/codex/claude/gemini) is the only writer and its build session is resumed across review rounds. Project specifics live in .metate/profile.yml; the skills are codebase-agnostic.

entries.title: cowork-to-code-bridge
entries.description: Connect Claude Cowork to Claude Code on your own computer (macOS or Linux). Cowork hands a task to a local Claude Code agent (and can run approved scripts too); the bridge is idempotent, streams progress, and is token-protected. Install with one command; it sets up a global Cowork skill so every session connects automatically — no /plugin, no setup inside Cowork.

entries.title: psotobverse-utils
entries.description: Claude-only orthogonal workflow layer: thin-verb skills (index, goal, reconcile, deliberate, tidy, reflect, audit-report) and commands (comprehend, coverage, cross-examine, sync-cowork) over a shared robust core; governance hooks (nothing-loose write guard, .env protection, doc hygiene, stop reminder); additive opt-in policy routing (policy_router reminders + comprehension_gate write-gate, driven by the project's .claude/policy/routing.yml; routing is additive, never subtractive); an opt-in meta-learner (SessionStart crash-detect + fast resume + environment probe, UserPromptSubmit signal capture, SessionEnd handoff; activated by .claude/meta-learner.json) and generator/auditor subagents (executor, explorer). /coverage is a recall safety net (decorrelated lens panel, union aggregation) against missing a relevant policy; /comprehend is a restate-before-act gate; /cross-examine is an adversarial review panel. Hooks and subagents run in both Claude Code and Cowork.

entries.title: esoteric-elucidation

entries.description: Perspective-driven, outline-first long-form research (Stanford STORM): multi-perspective research conversations, an evidence-grounded outline, a sectioned cited article, and a deterministic citation audit.

entries.title: session-cost
entries.description: Report token usage, wall-clock time, and per-model cost for Claude Code sessions by parsing the on-disk ~/.claude/projects JSONL transcripts — grouped by session or git worktree, with a compare mode. Dedups streamed/re-serialized usage and prices per model so totals reconcile with ccusage. Read-only.

entries.title: the-orchestrator
entries.description: Decompose a design doc into a parallelizable DAG of tasks and run them across git worktrees with an interactive visual workflow. Repo-agnostic.

entries.title: workflows
entries.description: Process and shipping skills: game jam, fast prototyping, Steam, itch.io.

entries.title: dopadone
entries.description: Two surfaces over the dopadone engine. (1) A health-rhythm interrupt: a configurable 4-phase sleep schedule (work → wrap-up → evening-routine → sleep) woven into Claude Code sessions, plus all-day probabilistic habit reminders. Work hours stay silent; wrap-up adds soft non-blocking 'close your loops' nudges; evening-routine refuses work and persuades you toward your own wind-down routine; sleep is a near-total refusal escapable only by the override phrase. Minute-granular HH:MM boundaries. (2) The `/do` skill: a slot-machine that picks the next single task from your unified pool and walks it research → brief → one action → record. Both call the `dopadone` CLI.

entries.title: scaffold-agent-team
entries.description: Scaffold a repo-coupled multi-agent team (lead / architect / backend / qa / devops) tuned to a target repo's conventions — emits project-local agents, slash commands, and a coordination protocol, then adversarially verifies the generated claims.

entries.title: dhh-vibecoding
entries.description: Vibecoding with the DHH philosophy. 17 skills distilled from 37signals' Fizzy, Campfire, and Writebook — zero build tools, no devise, no Sidekiq, no Redis. Teaches Claude Code to write Rails 8 + Hotwire + Solid Suite + Kamal apps the DHH way. Main skills cover PRD writing, Rails conventions, schema design (Socratic interrogation flow), Turbo, Stimulus, Tailwind, auth, Active Storage, Solid Suite, Kamal, Mayar payments, RubyLLM. Extras skills cover vanilla CSS (DHH-style) and advanced VPS hardening (Tailscale + Cloudflare).

entries.title: finance-skills
entries.description: Financial analysis and trading skills — options payoff charts, stock correlations, market data via yfinance, Twitter/Discord/Telegram research, and generative UI widgets.

entries.title: spawnforge
entries.description: SpawnForge Claude Code automation — agents, skills, hooks, and MCP for AI-native game engine development

entries.title: promptworld
entries.description: PromptWorld — the meta-compiler *World: a WrightMaster Engineer-CEO that has every skill plus a native subagent per component type (skill / mcp / harness / operating_system / prompt / team / workflow), and 7 PromptGym specialist AIOS agents, that compile / compose / publish prompt systems through the nomicon. Bundles the install-promptworld deploy WIZARD (the agent builds the docker image, boots the container running the Engineer-CEO + PromptGym + the assistant-ui chat / Monaco file explorer / embedded terminal, copies the Claude creds in, and hands the user one URL), compile-a-world (the meta-compiler skill — the nomicon folds on itself), and ingest-into-nomicon-app.

entries.title: session-sync
entries.description: Cross-device synchronization for Claude Code sessions and settings via iCloud Drive

entries.title: yakrover
entries.description: Yak Robotics — robot task auction marketplace. Discover robots, post survey tasks, run auctions, and settle payments via MCP.

entries.title: open-agreements
entries.description: Fill standard legal agreement templates and produce signable DOCX files. Supports Common Paper and Bonterms templates.

entries.title: claude-code-style-kit
entries.description: Reusable coding, testing, review, and TanStack Query conventions for consistent Claude Code output across projects.

entries.title: sq
entries.description: Agent skill for the sq CLI: SLQ and native SQL, sources and handles, output formats, inspect/diff/tbl, and per-driver notes for databases and file formats.

entries.title: career-search-tools

entries.description: Writing guidance based on William Strunk Jr.'s The Elements of Style (1918)

entries.description: Trinity VSA/FPGA development framework with custom skills, hooks, and MCP integration for ternary computing

entries.title: intent-mail
entries.description: Self-hosted email triage for Claude Code over Gmail and Outlook. Daily check-in digest (P1–P4 priority + why + needs-response), long-thread summaries, group-by-category, and real actions (read/archive/delete/draft/flag/move) through Gmail and Microsoft Graph — you hold the OAuth token, no third party sees your mailbox.

entries.title: kagura-memory
entries.description: Kagura Memory Cloud plugin — session management, memory recall/save, and setup tools

entries.title: autumn
entries.description: Domain knowledge, CLI wrappers, scaffolding commands, and code review for the autumn-web Rust framework

entries.title: uipath
entries.description: UiPath plugin for Claude Code — custom skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers for UiPath RPA workflows, UI automation, UI testing, Python coded agents and UiPath troubleshoot

entries.description: Continuous improvement plugin for Claude Code — enforcement hooks, reflection workflows, dev workflow skills, and recursive self-improvement

entries.title: mcp-oda
entries.description: Integration with Oda for searching products and managing your shopping cart.

entries.title: design-extractor
entries.description: Extract a complete, validated, reusable design system from any URL — patterns, relationships, motion, voice, and a self-validating shadcn/Tailwind HTML replica. Iteratively refines until the replica matches reference screenshots at >=0.85 similarity. Produces a per-brand DESIGN.md and an installable per-brand SKILL.md, indexed in a local design library at ~/.claude/design-library/.

entries.title: xentral-connector
entries.description: Xentral Agent OS — connector and skills.

entries.title: larch
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow automation for Claude Code: issue-anchored `/design` (one direct-drafting flow; plan-review panel), `/bug` (consumer issue-filing helper), `/deps` issue dependency audits, and `/implement` (positional `<issue-N>`, fixed 7200s Step 2 timeout, optional `--merge`, `--forked`, `--draft`, `--no-admin-fallback`, `--no-logs-commit`, `--coder`, `--run-id`). `/implement` Preflight reads the `larch:plan` block from the issue body; `/implement` Step 5 code review always uses the unified strict panel internally (public `--panel` argv removed).

entries.title: autonomous-fleet
entries.description: Portable multi-agent engineering framework for fully-autonomous coding runs across orchestration tools. A tool-agnostic core engine, per-runtime adapters (Claude Code, Codex, Grok, Orca), and 3 shipped mission skills (doc-sync, test-coverage, adversarial-review-and-fix) that compose into multi-step engineering campaigns; 18 more missions ship as exploratory docs pending real-run promotion.

entries.title: build-loop
entries.description: Multi-phase build orchestration with native debugging: assess, plan, execute, review, iterate. Plan-verify gate on Phase 2. Optional UI design gates. Native debug-loop, debugging-memory, and logging-tracer skills provide root-cause investigation without a bundled MCP server. Orchestrator owns when-to-fire; bundled skills own procedural detail; logging-tracer-bridge is an optional escalation hop to the standalone Coding Debugger supporting plugin for extended capability.

entries.title: codey
entries.description: Live legibility for Claude Code: narrates what Claude is doing and warns when it's stuck.

entries.title: trendvane
entries.description: daily trend research and content brief generator for creators. fetches from hackernews, reddit, tiktok, youtube, product hunt, and google trends, scores by niche fit, and drops 3 shootable video briefs into notion every morning. on first run it guides you through full setup.

entries.title: claudeclaw-plus
entries.description: ClaudeClaw+ — governance, orchestration, persistent memory, and hardened web UI for Claude Code daemons. Sister project to moazbuilds/claudeclaw.

entries.description: VX Universal memory for Claude Code. Connects to the hosted MCP endpoint over HTTP with OAuth — no API key needed.

entries.description: Multi-agent workflow system for Claude Code. Pipeline-based agent orchestration: Issue → Design → Plan → Implement → Review → PR.

entries.description: Search and compare free tiers, credits, and pricing changes across 1,500+ developer tools. Find deals for databases, hosting, CI/CD, monitoring, and 50+ categories.

entries.title: crucible-suite
entries.description: Complete writing system for epic fantasy novels using the Crucible Structureâ€”a 36-beat narrative framework with three interwoven story strands. Includes planning, outlining, prose writing with bi-chapter reviews, and editing.

entries.title: spec-to-pr
entries.description: Evidence-driven Claude Code plugin for turning briefs, Figma designs, and OpenAPI contracts into verified pull requests.

entries.title: nicopowers
entries.description: Nico's personal engineering-process skills: brainstorming, planning, TDD, systematic debugging, verification, code review, git worktrees, and skill authoring.

entries.title: lsp-refactor
entries.description: Project-wide rename, file-move-with-importer-updates, and move-symbol via a real language server. Use this whenever a refactor touches more than one file — Claude Code's built-in LSP tool is READ-ONLY and cannot rename, move files, or move symbols. A text-only edit silently misses re-exports, aliased and type-only imports, and {@link} references; this plugin drives the LSP to catch every one.

entries.title: andrej-karpathy-skills
entries.description: Behavioral guidelines to reduce common LLM coding mistakes, derived from Andrej Karpathy's observations on LLM coding pitfalls

entries.title: m3
entries.description: Local-first persistent memory for Claude Code — 101 MCP tools, hybrid search (FTS5 + vector + MMR), bitemporal contradictions, GDPR primitives, chatlog auto-capture, multi-agent handoffs.

entries.title: dwsd-md
entries.description: Read, write, and validate DWSD organization entities — the Dynamic Worksystems Design markdown format.

entries.title: polygram
entries.description: Telegram integration for Claude Code that preserves the OpenClaw per-chat session model. Migration target for OpenClaw users. Multi-bot, multi-chat, per-topic isolation; SQLite transcripts; inline-keyboard approvals. Bundles /polygram:status|logs|pair-code|approvals admin commands plus history (transcript queries) and polygram-send (out-of-turn IPC sends with file-upload validation) skills.

entries.title: harness-kit
entries.description: A portable Claude Code engineering harness — plan → work → verify → feedback discipline as reusable agents, skills, and hooks, plus an `introspect` skill that tailors a .claude/ setup to any repo's tech stack.

entries.title: web-search
entries.description: Web search, content extraction, and research tools for Claude Code. Search the web, Reddit, Hacker News, GitHub, X/Twitter, Wikipedia, arXiv, and more — all from your Claude session.

entries.title: second-brain
entries.description: Self-evolving AI second brain. Automatically learns from sessions, discovers tools, maintains a local knowledge base, and self-critiques code quality — getting smarter with every interaction.

entries.title: copilotkit
entries.description: AI agent skills for CopilotKit — setup, develop, integrate, debug, upgrade, and contribute to CopilotKit projects

entries.title: ml-odyssey-skills
entries.description: Skills migrated to ProjectMnemosyne for cross-project reuse

entries.description: Bioinformatics-native AI agent skill library — pharmacogenomics, ancestry, scRNA-seq, metagenomics, variant annotation, genome comparison, and more. 24 skills with deterministic Python execution, reproducibility bundles, and local-first privacy.

entries.description: Plugin-safe Claude Code distribution of Antigravity Awesome Skills with 1,396 supported skills.

entries.description: Opinionated skills for scaffolding Python projects with an Astral-centric toolchain (uv, ruff, ty), pre-commit, Renovate, CI and Docker.

entries.description: Expert prompt-engineering reference for AI agents on Claude, GPT, and Gemini APIs, plus authoring CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md project-instruction files

entries.title: udflow
entries.description: Universal Dev Flow — risk-proportional multi-agent engineering workflow: plan-gated implementation, selected read-only reviewers, gatekeeper readiness judgment, failure memory, and optional external capabilities (MCP / skills / subagents).

entries.description: 20 SEO/GEO skills and 5 commands on one shared contract for keyword research, content creation, technical audits, schema markup, monitoring, quality gates, entity truth, and campaign memory.

entries.title: agenthood-knowledge
entries.description: Knowledge and documentation skills: API references, ADRs, READMEs (the-librarian) and institutional knowledge about member format, naming conventions, and Society structure (the-oracle).

entries.title: kick
entries.description: Move a live Claude Code session between your laptop and your own server: hand it off so it survives closing the lid, drive it from your phone, and pull everything back. Code + uncommitted changes + conversation, at the exact position.

entries.title: skill-conductor
entries.description: Create, edit, evaluate, and package Claude Code agent skills — full draft→test→review→improve lifecycle.

entries.title: gabble
entries.description: Gabble — lazy dev + token optimizer. Combines ponytail's YAGNI ladder with aggressive token optimization: precision retrieval, context hygiene, output compression, and cache-aware batching. Less code, fewer tokens, same answers.

entries.title: omnitune
entries.description: Keep your prompts and skills in tune with the current best practices of whatever AI model you're running. Audits skills/agents and rewrites ad-hoc prompts; model-agnostic, repo-agnostic, self-updating. Open-sourced by Digital Research Group.

entries.title: caliber-analysis
entries.description: Analyzes how your team uses Claude Code. Captures session activity — prompts, responses, and tool use — with secrets redacted on your machine before anything is sent, then ships it to your Caliber tenant for AI-governance insights. Bearer-authenticated, idempotent, and non-blocking.

entries.title: luminaries
entries.description: Paired-thinker review lenses for design review and code audit. Each pair fuses two software-engineering thinkers into one stereo lens held in tension on an axis (e.g. structure × runtime); aim it at a design, spec, PR, diff, or repo. Routes by subject, stage, and intent; runs as a single best-fit pair, a multi-pair panel that fans out one lens per axis and collates, or a single-luminary anchor lookup.

entries.title: steakorder
entries.description: Adaptive requirements-elicitation gate. Asks one adaptive question at a time, screens every answer through four quality gates, and produces an ISO/IEC/IEEE 29148 Software Requirements Specification. Also regenerates a clean SRS from an existing requirements document.

entries.title: constellation
entries.description: Curated skills that work together for a complete dev workflow

entries.title: world-cup-oracle
entries.description: World Cup prediction plugin with deterministic Python probabilities, a lightweight provenance-aware data hub, and an isolated national-team model lab for candidate training and walk-forward evaluation. Models remain provisional unless promotion gates pass.

entries.title: mollie
entries.description: Mollie payment integration — connects Claude to your Mollie account via the Mollie MCP server and activates integration skills for payments, Mollie Components, webhooks, and Mollie Connect.

entries.title: pxp
entries.description: Pivotal Labs–style XP pairing workflow: epic refinement, story slicing, EARS specifications, and strict per-spec TDD with developer approval at every gate.

entries.title: undertone
entries.description: On-device audio transcripts you own. Ingest local files, YouTube, podcasts, RSS, Quill, or Google Meet, run local ASR, diarization, and speaker fingerprinting, then browse, search, and export. One skill that routes to a reference per surface.

entries.title: reverse-prompt-engineer
entries.description: Reverse-engineer the prompt that most likely produced a given piece of text, via a fan-out guess -> execute (clean room) -> score -> steer workflow with verbatim-leakage and length penalties.

entries.title: karpathy-code-guidelines
entries.description: Six Karpathy-inspired principles that stop Claude Code from making silent assumptions, overengineering, doing drive-by refactoring, and patching bugs blindly.

entries.title: toolkit-mcp-server
entries.description: Fast developer utilities — random ID generation, QR codes, IP geolocation, hashing, and encoding — plus gated, fail-closed network and system diagnostics.

entries.title: epik
entries.description: Manager-mode feature development on GitHub: converge in CoWork, author the issue graph, launch autonomous builds on Claude Code on the web.

entries.title: ios-pro
entries.description: Senior Apple platform review — architecture, security, privacy, SwiftUI, Swift concurrency, accessibility, performance. iOS 18+ / Swift 6.2+.

entries.description: Duplex companion for AI agent CLIs — keep talking while the agent thinks, and recall every conversation. Skill: recall Delphin's local memory, or build/run the wrapper.

entries.description: 프론트엔드 개발을 위한 개인 Claude Code 하네스

entries.description: Query a Svelte/SvelteKit component dependency graph over MCP — what imports what, unused components, route→component maps, and a component's Svelte 5 props/slots. Bundles the MCP server and the /svelte-graph skill.

entries.description: Source-attribution injection + mechanical grounding verification. Makes Claude Code cite machine-checkable artifacts (file:line, tool calls) and verifies those citations against the current filesystem; warn-only by default.

entries.title: dev-proposal-toolkit
entries.description: Web・システム開発案件の受注提案を一気通貫でサポートするスキルセット。市場調査→技術設計→UI/UX設計→見積もり→提案書作成までをカバー。

entries.title: ai-geo-by-ivan
entries.description: GEO / AI-search optimization toolkit by Aurelius Ivan Wijaya. A fork of Corey Haines's marketing-skills (CRO, copywriting, SEO, AI SEO, paid ads, ad creative, growth) extended with Ivan's own GEO skills: geo-audit (full AI-search audit + client-ready graph report) and index-on-search-engine (register a site/subdomain on Google + Bing).

entries.title: arc
entries.description: The full arc from idea to shipped code. Lifecycle workflows for vision, ideation, implementation, review, testing, audit, launch, and commits.
